title: French PM Lecornu warns of 'fairly severe' wildfires arriving unusually early
subhead: According to the prime minister, the wildfire season is running at least two weeks ahead of its usual schedule. With vegetation already extremely dry, he voiced concerns about the 'endurance' of firefighting resources.

title: Heat dome roasts eastern US ahead of Fourth of July celebrations

Germany seeks to introduce a dose of Nordic-style 'flexicurity' into its labor law
subhead: Through a series of reform plans, Berlin has committed to easing dismissal rules alongside measures to support job transitions, aiming to reduce the cost of failure for young companies.

title: 250 years of American independence: 'Enslaved and freed Black people were not merely witnesses to the Atlantic Revolutions, they were active participants'
subhead: Elisabeth Landi, a history professor, argues in this op-ed for Le Monde that commemorations marking the birth of the United States should fully acknowledge the memory of Black fighters who shed their blood for a revolution that did not challenge the institution of slavery.

title: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' sparks a Homeric debate in Greece
subhead: The casting of a Black actress as Helen of Troy has ignited fierce controversy in Greece. Some lament what they see as Hollywood's appropriation of Greek mythology, while others emphasize the universal nature of these ancient stories.

title: Economist Gita Gopinath: 'Rising imbalances among major powers threaten growth and fuel protectionism'
subhead: Harvard University economics professor and former International Monetary Fund official Gita Gopinath expresses concern over the level of US federal debt.

subhead: A new generation of candidates, critical of elites, social injustice and US support for Israel, is making its mark in the Democratic primaries ahead of November's midterm elections.

title: Lebanon's Shiites, torn over Hezbollah, question the meaning of their sacrifices
subhead: Days after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement, supporters and opponents of the militia-party are still measuring the consequences of the devastating war.

title: 'We should not accept that heatwaves cause thousands of deaths. That is not normal'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Dossier, founder of the environmental consulting firm Quattrolibri, offers an initial political assessment of how France responded to the most intense heatwave ever recorded in the country.
